ddls:
CREATE TABLE "social_media_platforms" (
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each social media platform */,
  "platform_name" TEXT /* Name of the social media platform */,
  "platform_url" TEXT /* URL of the social media platform */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "platform_id" INTEGER /* ID of the social media platform where the tweet was posted */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was posted */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets for the tweet */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes for the tweet */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Engagement score indicating the engagement generated by the tweet */,
  "tweet_url" TEXT /* URL of the tweet */,
  "reply_count" INTEGER /* Number of replies to the tweet */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet was quoted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_platform_id FOREIGN KEY ("platform_id") REFERENCES social_media_platforms ("platform_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "followers" INTEGER /* Number of followers for the user */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  "user_description" TEXT /* Description of the user's profile */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Whether the user is verified or not */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_symbols" (
  "stock_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stock symbol */,
  "stock_symbol" TEXT /* Stock symbol (e.g., $AMZN, $TSLA) */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Company name associated with the stock symbol */,
  "sector" TEXT /* Sector of the company */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry of the company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stock_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tweet_stock_mentions" (
  "tweet_stock_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet-stock mention */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that mentions the stock symbol */,
  "stock_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stock symbol mentioned in the tweet */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score indicating the sentiment of the tweet for the stock symbol */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_stock_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_stock_mentions_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweet_stock_mentions_stock_id FOREIGN KEY ("stock_id") REFERENCES stock_symbols ("stock_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet that was analyzed */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score (-1 to 1) indicating the sentiment of the tweet */,
  "sentiment_label" TEXT /* Sentiment label (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) indicating the sentiment of the tweet */,
  "subjectivity_score" REAL /* Subjectivity score indicating the subjectivity of the tweet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "influencers" (
  "influencer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each influencer */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is an influencer */,
  "influence_score" REAL /* Influence score indicating the influence of the user */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Topic of the influencer's expertise */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("influencer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_influencers_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "news_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each news event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the news event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Description of the news event */,
  "impact_score" REAL /* Impact score indicating the impact of the news event on the stock market */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stock_market_data" (
  "stock_id" INTEGER /* ID of the stock */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the market data */,
  "open_price" REAL /* Opening price of the stock on the given date */,
  "high_price" REAL /* Highest price of the stock on the given date */,
  "low_price" REAL /* Lowest price of the stock on the given date */,
  "close_price" REAL /* Closing price of the stock on the given date */,
  "volume" INTEGER /* Trading volume of the stock on the given date */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stock_id", "date")
)
